name: Close Window
desc: Player closes an inventory window (chest, crafting table, furnace, etc).

fields.name: windowId
fields.desc: Window ID of the container being closed

notes: Rise 6.0 CancelInventoryMove cancels C0D to keep the inventory 'open' and allow WASD movement. RektSky B5 ChestStealer sends C0D after stealing. Jigsaw 0.26 InventoryPlus cancels C0D. Sigma 4.11 InventoryWalk cancels C0D.

implementation.overview: C0DPacketCloseWindow tells the server the player closed an inventory screen. Window ID 0 = player inventory, other IDs = chests/tables. Exploited by: CancelInventoryMove (cancel C0D to allow WASD move with open GUI), ChestStealer (send C0D when done stealing to auto-close), and InventoryWalk (cancel C0D to keep inventory open while moving).

modules.name: CancelInventoryMove (Rise 6.0)
modules.purpose: Cancel C0DPacketCloseWindow to keep the inventory GUI technically open on the server, allowing WASD movement while the player has a screen open. Combined with cancelling C0B OPEN_INVENTORY and C16 OPEN_INVENTORY_ACHIEVEMENT.
modules.how_it_works: The PacketSendEvent listener cancels three packet types: C16PacketClientStatus(OPEN_INVENTORY_ACHIEVEMENT) to prevent the server from hearing the inventory open, C0BPacketEntityAction(OPEN_INVENTORY) for the same, and C0DPacketCloseWindow to prevent the server from hearing the inventory close. As far as the server knows, the player never had an inventory open at all — so WASD movement is never blocked by the 'can't move with GUI open' server rule.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayServer.processCloseWindow(C0DPacketCloseWindow) closes the player's open container. When cancelled, the server thinks the inventory is still open and does not allow movement. The PreUpdateEvent handler in CancelInventoryMove re-presses movement keys so the client keeps moving despite the open GUI.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Cancelling C0D while moving with an open inventory is detectable by anti-cheats that track GUI state (e.g., checking if the player moved while a container was open). Watchdog and Grim both flag 'inventory move' by checking that the player's C03 packets show movement while server-side container state is non-null. Cancelling C16 + C0B + C0D together desyncs the inventory state entirely.

modules.name: ChestStealer Close (RektSky B5)
modules.purpose: After all items in a chest are stolen, send C0DPacketCloseWindow to auto-close the chest GUI. The player doesn't need to press E or ESC.
modules.how_it_works: ChestStealer tracks the chest container via S2DPacketOpenWindow. After shift-clicking all items (C0EPacketClickWindow), it sends C0DPacketCloseWindow with the stored window ID. The auto-close is instant or delayed depending on the mode.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayServer.processCloseWindow(C0DPacketCloseWindow) — closes container and confirms via S2EPacketCloseWindow.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: ChestStealer auto-closing is not flagged by anti-cheats. Legitimate players close chests after looting.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client: EntityPlayerSP.closeScreen() sends C0D. Rise 6.0 hooks via PacketSendEvent. RektSky B5 sends C0D in ChestStealer. Sigma 4.11 InventoryWalk cancels C0D.

Server: NetHandlerPlayServer.processCloseWindow(C0DPacketCloseWindow) closes the container. The server sends S2EPacketCloseWindow back.
implementation.client_variations:
Rise 6.0: CancelInventoryMove cancels C0D + C16 + C0B for inventory movement. Manager.java sends C0D after auto-managing inventory.
RektSky B5: ChestStealer sends C0D after all items stolen.
Jigsaw 0.26: InventoryPlus cancels C0D.
Sigma 4.11: InventoryWalk cancels C0D for carry mode.
PingSpoofComponent (Rise) delays C0D packets.
